lines.english: Lesson 26
lines.chinese: 第26课
lines.raw: Lesson 26 | 第26课

lines.english: The past life of the earth
lines.chinese: 地球上的过去生命
lines.raw: The past life of the earth | 地球上的过去生命

lines.english: What is the main condition for the preservation of the remains of any living creature?
lines.chinese: 任何生物遗骸得以保存的主要条件是什么？
lines.raw: What is the main condition for the preservation of the remains of any living creature? | 任何生物遗骸得以保存的主要条件是什么？

lines.english: It is animals and plants which lived in or near water whose remains are most likely to be preserved,
lines.chinese: 生活在水里或水边的动植物遗骸最有可能被保存下来，
lines.raw: It is animals and plants which lived in or near water whose remains are most likely to be preserved, | 生活在水里或水边的动植物遗骸最有可能被保存下来，

lines.english: for one of the necessary conditions of preservation is quick burial,
lines.chinese: 因为保存的必要条件之一是快速掩埋，
lines.raw: for one of the necessary conditions of preservation is quick burial, | 因为保存的必要条件之一是快速掩埋，

lines.english: and it is only in the seas and rivers, and sometimes lakes,
lines.chinese: 而只有在海洋、河流以及有时湖泊中，
lines.raw: and it is only in the seas and rivers, and sometimes lakes, | 而只有在海洋、河流以及有时湖泊中，

lines.english: where mud and silt have been continuously deposited,
lines.chinese: 泥沙不断沉积的地方，
lines.raw: where mud and silt have been continuously deposited, | 泥沙不断沉积的地方，

lines.english: that bodies and the like can be rapidly covered over and preserved.
lines.chinese: 尸体和类似的东西才能迅速被覆盖并保存下来。
lines.raw: that bodies and the like can be rapidly covered over and preserved. | 尸体和类似的东西才能迅速被覆盖并保存下来。

lines.english: But even in the most favourable circumstances
lines.chinese: 但即使在最有利的条件下，
lines.raw: But even in the most favourable circumstances | 但即使在最有利的条件下，

lines.english: only a small fraction of the creatures that die are preserved in this way
lines.chinese: 只有一小部分死去的生物能以这种方式保存下来，
lines.raw: only a small fraction of the creatures that die are preserved in this way | 只有一小部分死去的生物能以这种方式保存下来，

lines.english: before decay sets in or, even more likely, before scavengers eat them.
lines.chinese: 在腐烂发生之前，或者更有可能，在食腐动物吃掉它们之前。
lines.raw: before decay sets in or, even more likely, before scavengers eat them. | 在腐烂发生之前，或者更有可能，在食腐动物吃掉它们之前。

lines.english: After all, all living creatures live by feeding on something else,
lines.chinese: 毕竟，所有生物都靠捕食其他生物而生，
lines.raw: After all, all living creatures live by feeding on something else, | 毕竟，所有生物都靠捕食其他生物而生，

lines.english: whether it be plant or animal, dead or alive,
lines.chinese: 无论是植物还是动物，死的还是活的，
lines.raw: whether it be plant or animal, dead or alive, | 无论是植物还是动物，死的还是活的，

lines.english: and it is only by chance that such a fate is avoided.
lines.chinese: 只有偶然才能避免这种命运。
lines.raw: and it is only by chance that such a fate is avoided. | 只有偶然才能避免这种命运。

lines.english: The remains of plants and animals that lived on land are much more rarely preserved,
lines.chinese: 生活在陆地上的动植物遗骸保存下来的情况要少得多，
lines.raw: The remains of plants and animals that lived on land are much more rarely preserved, | 生活在陆地上的动植物遗骸保存下来的情况要少得多，

lines.english: for there is seldom anything to cover them over.
lines.chinese: 因为很少有什么东西能覆盖它们。
lines.raw: for there is seldom anything to cover them over. | 因为很少有什么东西能覆盖它们。

lines.english: When you think of the innumerable birds that one sees flying about,
lines.chinese: 当你想到无数飞来飞去的鸟儿，
lines.raw: When you think of the innumerable birds that one sees flying about, | 当你想到无数飞来飞去的鸟儿，

lines.english: not to mention the equally numerous small animals like field mice and voles which you do not see,
lines.chinese: 更不用说你看不见的同样多的像田鼠和田鼠这样的小动物，
lines.raw: not to mention the equally numerous small animals like field mice and voles which you do not see, | 更不用说你看不见的同样多的像田鼠和田鼠这样的小动物，

lines.english: it is very rarely that one comes across a dead body, except, of course, on the roads.
lines.chinese: 你很少会碰到一具尸体，当然除了在路上。
lines.raw: it is very rarely that one comes across a dead body, except, of course, on the roads. | 你很少会碰到一具尸体，当然除了在路上。

lines.english: They decompose and are quickly destroyed by the weather or eaten by some other creature.
lines.chinese: 它们会分解，很快被天气破坏或被其他生物吃掉。
lines.raw: They decompose and are quickly destroyed by the weather or eaten by some other creature. | 它们会分解，很快被天气破坏或被其他生物吃掉。

lines.english: It is almost always due to some very special circumstances that traces of land animals survive,
lines.chinese: 陆地动物的痕迹几乎总是由于某些非常特殊的情况而得以幸存，
lines.raw: It is almost always due to some very special circumstances that traces of land animals survive, | 陆地动物的痕迹几乎总是由于某些非常特殊的情况而得以幸存，

lines.english: as by falling into inaccessible caves, or into an ice crevasse,
lines.chinese: 比如掉进难以进入的洞穴，或者掉进冰裂缝，
lines.raw: as by falling into inaccessible caves, or into an ice crevasse, | 比如掉进难以进入的洞穴，或者掉进冰裂缝，

lines.english: like the Siberian mammoths,
lines.chinese: 就像西伯利亚猛犸象一样，
lines.raw: like the Siberian mammoths, | 就像西伯利亚猛犸象一样，

lines.english: when the whole animal is sometimes preserved, as in a refrigerator.
lines.chinese: 有时整个动物都被保存下来，就像放在冰箱里一样。
lines.raw: when the whole animal is sometimes preserved, as in a refrigerator. | 有时整个动物都被保存下来，就像放在冰箱里一样。

lines.english: This is what happened to the famous Beresovka mammoth which was found preserved and in good condition.
lines.chinese: 著名的别列佐夫卡猛犸象就是这样，它被发现时保存完好。
lines.raw: This is what happened to the famous Beresovka mammoth which was found preserved and in good condition. | 著名的别列佐夫卡猛犸象就是这样，它被发现时保存完好。

lines.english: In his mouth were the remains of fir trees--the last meal that he had before he fell into the crevasse and broke his back.
lines.chinese: 它的嘴里有杉树的残骸——这是它掉进裂缝摔断背之前的最后一餐。
lines.raw: In his mouth were the remains of fir trees--the last meal that he had before he fell into the crevasse and broke his back. | 它的嘴里有杉树的残骸——这是它掉进裂缝摔断背之前的最后一餐。

lines.english: The mammoth has now been restored in the Palaeontological Museum in St.Petersburg.
lines.chinese: 这头猛犸象现已在圣彼得堡的古生物博物馆修复。
lines.raw: The mammoth has now been restored in the Palaeontological Museum in St.Petersburg. | 这头猛犸象现已在圣彼得堡的古生物博物馆修复。

lines.english: Other animals were trapped in tar pits, like the elephants, sabre-toothed cats,
lines.chinese: 其他动物被困在沥青坑里，比如大象、剑齿虎，
lines.raw: Other animals were trapped in tar pits, like the elephants, sabre-toothed cats, | 其他动物被困在沥青坑里，比如大象、剑齿虎，

lines.english: and numerous other creatures that are found at Rancho la Brea, which is now just a suburb of Los Angeles.
lines.chinese: 以及在拉布雷亚牧场发现的无数其他生物，那里现在只是洛杉矶的一个郊区。
lines.raw: and numerous other creatures that are found at Rancho la Brea, which is now just a suburb of Los Angeles. | 以及在拉布雷亚牧场发现的无数其他生物，那里现在只是洛杉矶的一个郊区。

lines.english: Apparently what happened was that water collected on these tar pits
lines.chinese: 显然，这些沥青坑上积水了，
lines.raw: Apparently what happened was that water collected on these tar pits | 显然，这些沥青坑上积水了，

lines.english: and the bigger animals like the elephants ventured out on to the apparently firm surface to drink,
lines.chinese: 像大象这样的大型动物冒险走到看似坚实的表面去饮水，
lines.raw: and the bigger animals like the elephants ventured out on to the apparently firm surface to drink, | 像大象这样的大型动物冒险走到看似坚实的表面去饮水，

lines.english: and were promptly bogged in the tar.
lines.chinese: 随即被困在沥青中。
lines.raw: and were promptly bogged in the tar. | 随即被困在沥青中。

lines.english: And then, when they were dead, the carnivores,
lines.chinese: 然后，当它们死去后，食肉动物，
lines.raw: And then, when they were dead, the carnivores, | 然后，当它们死去后，食肉动物，

lines.english: like the sabretoothed cats and the giant wolves,
lines.chinese: 比如剑齿虎和巨型狼，
lines.raw: like the sabretoothed cats and the giant wolves, | 比如剑齿虎和巨型狼，

lines.english: came out to feed and suffered exactly the same fate.
lines.chinese: 出来捕食，也遭受了同样的命运。
lines.raw: came out to feed and suffered exactly the same fate. | 出来捕食，也遭受了同样的命运。

lines.english: There are also endless numbers of birds in the tar as well.
lines.chinese: 沥青中也有无数的鸟类。
lines.raw: There are also endless numbers of birds in the tar as well. | 沥青中也有无数的鸟类。
